name: deploy
context: Deploy one pnpm workspace package into a self-contained target directory for servers, containers, or artifacts
info: This skill covers `pnpm deploy`, the workspace-oriented command that copies one package and the dependencies it needs into a target directory. It matters because monorepo automation often needs to ship one application without copying the entire repository, and pnpm deploy has important requirements around filters, legacy mode, and which dependency groups are included. The skill also ties into `pnpm fetch` and offline install patterns used in Docker and CI.
triggers: deploy one package from a pnpm monorepo
create a deploy directory with pnpm
ship a pnpm workspace app to docker
use pnpm deploy with legacy mode
body:
# pnpm Deploy

`pnpm deploy` is for extracting one workspace package into a portable target directory. The local CLI help for pnpm 10.23.0 shows the core form clearly:

```bash
pnpm --filter=<project> deploy <target-directory>
```

Useful flags include `--prod`, `--dev`, `--no-optional`, and `--legacy`. In practice, an agent should require a filter, decide whether devDependencies belong in the output, and fall back to legacy mode when the workspace is not using newer injected deployment behavior.

sections.title: Deploy command basics
sections.body: The official help text for pnpm 10.x describes `pnpm --filter=<deployed project name> deploy <target directory>`. Always select the package explicitly with `--filter`. Use `--prod` for runtime output, `--dev` for build-oriented bundles, and `--no-optional` when optional dependencies should not ship. If nothing matches the selector, add `--fail-if-no-match` before deploy to make the failure explicit.

sections.title: Docker and CI workflow
sections.body: A common pattern is to warm the store with [[pnpm/install#fetch-and-offline]], run an offline install, build the target package, and then deploy it to an output directory copied into the final image or artifact. This reduces network work inside later layers and keeps deployment scope narrow. Pair this with [[pnpm/store#caching-in-ci]] for cache placement.

sections.title: Legacy mode and file inclusion
sections.body: As newer pnpm versions evolved deploy behavior around workspace package injection, `--legacy` remains the escape hatch for older repos or setups that are not compatible with the default path. The `deployAllFiles` setting can also widen which files are copied; otherwise file inclusion follows the package's normal packlist behavior such as `files` and related manifest rules.

scripts.name: Deploy Workspace Package

filter.description: Workspace selector for the package being deployed

destination.description: Target directory passed to pnpm deploy

depends.reason: Deploy depends on correct workspace selection and graph scoping
Most deploy workflows are preceded by fetch, install, and build steps
